mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns as liquidity conditions improve.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 55 percent of observations when unemployment exceeds 7.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and retail sales indicates a synchronized contraction in economic activity, which forces equity valuations lower as revenue expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns in over 60 percent of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: During deep recessions, sharp drops in industrial production trigger aggressive central bank liquidity injections that override fundamental weakness and produce rapid equity market rebounds.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to decline for more than 10 trading days following industrial production drops below 95 percent, or the signal fails during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate demand and corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings growth slows.
failure_condition: Payroll declines coincide with positive forward returns in more than 58 percent of observations, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.05 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When existing home sales fall below 600 thousand units, housing-related financial distress intensifies and consumer wealth effects turn negative, pushing equity markets lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in over 65 percent of months when home sales drop below 600 thousand, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy after 2012.

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero interest rates combined with rapidly rising unemployment signal a liquidity trap where monetary policy loses transmission power, causing equity markets to price in prolonged stagnation.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive forward returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In deflationary recessions, falling GDP growth alongside stable or declining inflation indicates weak demand rather than supply shocks, prompting equity markets to anticipate prolonged economic contraction.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 15 trading days following concurrent GDP declines and CPI stability, or the signal fails to predict returns during inflationary expansions.
